# flashblade_certificate (Resource)

Manages a FlashBlade TLS certificate (import-mode: PEM certificate with private key).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "flashblade_certificate" "example" {
  name        = "my-tls-cert"
  certificate = file("${path.module}/cert.pem")
  private_key = file("${path.module}/key.pem")

  # Optional
  # intermediate_certificate = file("${path.module}/chain.pem")
  # passphrase               = var.cert_passphrase
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `certificate` (String) The PEM-encoded X.509 certificate body.
- `name` (String) The name of the certificate. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `certificate_type` (String) The certificate type (e.g. appliance, external). Defaults to 'appliance' when not specified.
- `intermediate_certificate` (String) The PEM-encoded intermediate certificate chain.
- `passphrase` (String, Sensitive) The passphrase protecting the private key. Not returned by the API after creation.
- `private_key` (String, Sensitive) The PEM-encoded private key. Not returned by the API after creation.
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `common_name` (String) The common name (CN) extracted from the certificate.
- `country` (String) The country (C) field extracted from the certificate.
- `email` (String) The email address extracted from the certificate.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the certificate.
- `issued_by` (String) The issuer of the certificate. Changes when the certificate is renewed.
- `issued_to` (String) The subject of the certificate. Changes when the certificate is renewed.
- `key_algorithm` (String) The key algorithm (e.g. RSA, EC). Changes when the certificate is renewed.
- `key_size` (Number) The key size in bits. Changes when the certificate is renewed.
- `locality` (String) The locality (L) field extracted from the certificate.
- `organization` (String) The organization (O) field extracted from the certificate.
- `organizational_unit` (String) The organizational unit (OU) field extracted from the certificate.
- `state` (String) The state/province (ST) field extracted from the certificate.
- `status` (String) The certificate status (e.g. imported, self-signed). Changes when the certificate is renewed.
- `subject_alternative_names` (List of String) The subject alternative names (SANs) extracted from the certificate.
- `valid_from` (Number) The Unix timestamp (milliseconds) from which the certificate is valid. Changes when renewed.
- `valid_to` (Number) The Unix timestamp (milliseconds) until which the certificate is valid. Changes when renewed.

<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Read operations occur during any refresh or planning operation when refresh is enabled.
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import flashblade_certificate.example my-tls-cert
```
